refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall provide an integrated capability to derive performance requirements from worksite mission context (site conditions, task objectives, safety constraints), record derived requirements with traceability to source inputs, and maintain a managed baseline history including calibration updates and performance target revisions.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates three distinct functional concerns: derivation (analysis/synthesis), recording (data capture), and management (lifecycle control). Refinement separates these into analyzable sub-functions while maintaining traceability to worksite inputs and baseline management as acceptance criteria. High safety criticality demands explicit linkage to safety constraints in derivation logic.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall demonstrate: (1) a documented derivation method that maps worksite inputs (site conditions, task type, safety plan) to performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability); (2) a recorded baseline with full traceability to source inputs; (3) a change history showing at least two calibration/update cycles with audit trail; (4) confirmation that safety-critical performance targets are flagged and reviewed by Safety Manager.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Derive performance requirements from worksite mission context inputs (site conditions, task objectives, safety constraints) using a documented method.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Record derived performance requirements with full traceability to source worksite inputs and derivation assumptions.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Maintain a managed baseline history of performance targets, including calibration updates, revisions, and audit trail.
CR-NGHE-005.4: Ensure safety-critical performance requirements are identified, validated by Safety Manager, and linked to worksite safety plan.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized performance requirement management system with integrated derivation engine, baseline versioning, and traceability database; all logic and data in single NGHE Solution module.

DP-B: Distributed derivation logic (Vehicle-resident heuristics) feeding into centralized NGHE Solution baseline manager; teleoperation provides operator input; Safety Services validates safety-critical targets.

DP-C: Federated architecture where Vehicle maintains local performance model, Teleoperation provides mission context and operator adjustments, NGHE Solution aggregates and manages baselines, Safety Services enforces safety constraints via SafetyAlert interface.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Federated architecture with role-based derivation and validation. Vehicle maintains a local performance model (cycle time, energy efficiency estimates based on equipment state and task type). Teleoperation module accepts mission context inputs (site conditions, task objectives) and operator adjustments, transmitting these via Tasking interface. NGHE Solution aggregates inputs, applies derivation logic to synthesize performance targets, maintains baseline versions, and records traceability. Safety Services validates safety-critical targets against worksite safety plan and returns SafetyAlert signals for non-compliant targets. All baseline history and audit trails are recorded in NGHE Solution.

potential_sources: Vehicle performance model must be synchronized with NGHE Solution baseline (mitigated by Telemetry interface providing real-time state).
Teleoperation mission context input timing must align with derivation cycle (mitigated by explicit Tasking interface with timestamp/version control).
Safety Services validation latency could delay baseline approval (mitigated by asynchronous SafetyAlert with escalation protocol).
coupling_check.notes: DP-C exhibits loose coupling via well-defined interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert). Each module owns its domain logic (Vehicle: performance estimation; Teleoperation: mission input; NGHE Solution: aggregation/baseline; Safety Services: constraint validation). Decoupling is achieved through interface contracts rather than shared data structures.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Vehicle can reliably estimate performance parameters (cycle time, energy efficiency) from onboard sensors and task type.
ASSUMPTION: Teleoperation module has access to complete worksite mission context (site conditions, task objectives, operator intent).
ASSUMPTION: Safety Services has access to worksite safety plan and can validate performance targets within acceptable latency (<5 min for non-real-time derivation).
RISK: If Vehicle performance model is inaccurate or stale, derived targets may be unrealistic; mitigation: periodic calibration against actual performance data.
RISK: If Teleoperation mission context is incomplete or ambiguous, derivation may produce invalid targets; mitigation: structured input schema with validation.
RISK: Safety Services validation failure could block baseline updates; mitigation: escalation protocol and manual override with audit trail.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-C is sound but requires explicit specification of: (1) derivation algorithm (e.g., weighted combination of Vehicle estimates and historical baselines); (2) interface schemas for Tasking (mission context structure) and SafetyAlert (validation result format); (3) baseline versioning and rollback procedures; (4) calibration trigger conditions and frequency.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Owns the performance requirement derivation orchestration, baseline management, traceability database, and audit trail. Acts as the central aggregator and decision authority for performance targets. Interfaces with Vehicle (via Telemetry), Teleoperation (via Tasking), and Safety Services (via SafetyAlert) to collect inputs, validate constraints, and record outcomes.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle transmits real-time performance state (cycle time, energy consumption, availability metrics) and equipment status to NGHE Solution for baseline calibration and performance model updates.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Teleoperation transmits mission context (site conditions, task objectives, operator adjustments) to NGHE Solution for performance requirement derivation.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution sends derived safety-critical performance targets to Safety Services for validation; Safety Services returns SafetyAlert signals indicating compliance or non-compliance with worksite safety plan.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution communicates with remote systems (e.g., Site Manager dashboard, maintenance systems) for baseline distribution, historical queries, and audit trail access.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural owner of baseline management, traceability, and audit trail. Derivation orchestration (aggregating inputs from Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) is logically central. Minimal diffusion: only interface contracts leak to other modules.

Vehicle could own local performance model estimation, but baseline management and traceability must remain in NGHE Solution. If Vehicle owned derivation, baseline history would be fragmented across equipment instances, violating single source of truth. High diffusion risk.

Teleoperation provides mission context input but should not own derivation or baseline management. Operator-centric module; performance requirement management is system-level concern. High diffusion risk if baseline logic embedded in teleoperation UI.

Safety Services owns constraint validation (SafetyAlert) but not baseline management. If Safety Services owned derivation, it would conflate safety validation with performance optimization, violating separation of concerns. Moderate diffusion risk.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the optimal owner because: (1) it is the system-level integrator with visibility across all inputs (Vehicle telemetry, Teleoperation mission context, Safety Services constraints); (2) baseline management and audit trail are system-level concerns requiring centralized authority; (3) traceability to worksite inputs demands a single source of truth; (4) performance requirement derivation is a cross-cutting concern that should not be embedded in domain-specific modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services).
containment_risks: RISK: NGHE Solution becomes a monolithic aggregator if derivation logic is not modularized internally; mitigation: decompose derivation into sub-modules (e.g., performance model engine, baseline manager, traceability engine) within NGHE Solution.
RISK: Tight coupling between NGHE Solution and Safety Services if SafetyAlert interface is not well-defined; mitigation: explicit interface contract with timeout and escalation protocol.

RISK: Performance requirement derivation latency could impact operational responsiveness if NGHE Solution is not sufficiently responsive; mitigation: asynchronous derivation with caching and incremental updates.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: IM assignment is sound. Recommend: (1) define internal sub-module structure within NGHE Solution (e.g., Derivation Engine, Baseline Manager, Traceability Engine) to avoid monolithic design; (2) specify interface schemas and SLAs for Telemetry, Tasking, and SafetyAlert; (3) establish baseline versioning and rollback procedures; (4) define audit trail retention policy and access controls.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (federated architecture) directly addresses all sub-FRs: (1) Vehicle + Teleoperation + NGHE Solution collectively derive requirements from worksite context; (2) NGHE Solution records with traceability via aggregation of Telemetry and Tasking inputs; (3) NGHE Solution maintains baseline history; (4) Safety Services validates safety-critical targets. No independence violations detected; each DP element maps to distinct functional concern. Coupling is managed via well-defined interfaces.

diffusion_risks: If NGHE Solution derivation logic is not internally modularized, future changes to derivation algorithm could require changes across multiple sub-modules, increasing diffusion.
If Telemetry, Tasking, or SafetyAlert interface contracts are not rigorously enforced, other modules may attempt to bypass NGHE Solution and directly manipulate baselines, fragmenting authority.
If baseline versioning logic is embedded in multiple places (e.g., Vehicle, Teleoperation, NGHE Solution), audit trail integrity could be compromised.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution containment is strong (0.95 score). Ownership is clear and unambiguous. Interface boundaries are well-defined. Recommended mitigation: establish strict interface governance and internal modularization within NGHE Solution to prevent future diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible via: (1) traceability matrix mapping worksite inputs to derived performance targets; (2) baseline history audit trail with timestamps and change records; (3) test scenarios exercising derivation with various worksite contexts; (4) Safety Manager sign-off on safety-critical targets. All verification artifacts can be produced by NGHE Solution module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution owns derivation orchestration, recording, and baseline management. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services provide inputs/validation via defined interfaces. Acceptance criteria (documented performance targets, traceability, baseline history) are directly traceable to NGHE Solution outputs.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-C to specify derivation algorithm (e.g., weighted combination of Vehicle estimates, historical baselines, and operator adjustments).
Define interface schemas for Tasking (mission context structure) and SafetyAlert (validation result format) with version control.
Establish baseline versioning procedure (e.g., semantic versioning, rollback protocol) and audit trail retention policy.
Define calibration trigger conditions (e.g., performance deviation >10%, periodic recalibration every N cycles) and frequency.
Specify Safety Manager review and approval workflow for safety-critical performance targets.
Establish internal sub-module structure within NGHE Solution to prevent monolithic design (e.g., Derivation Engine, Baseline Manager, Traceability Engine).

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-005 has been successfully refined into four atomic sub-FRs (CR-NGHE-005.1 through CR-NGHE-005.4), each addressing a distinct functional concern (derivation, recording, management, safety validation). DP-C (federated architecture) is sound and well-coupled. NGHE Solution is the clear IM owner with strong containment (0.95). All three gates (FR-DP, DP-IM, FR-IM) pass. However, the sub-FRs require further decomposition to reach atomic, implementable requirements. Recommend proceeding to next decomposition level for each sub-FR.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Derive performance requirements from worksite mission context inputs (site conditions, task objectives, safety constraints) using a documented method.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Record derived performance requirements with full traceability to source worksite inputs and derivation assumptions.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Maintain a managed baseline history of performance targets, including calibration updates, revisions, and audit trail.
CR-NGHE-005.4: Ensure safety-critical performance requirements are identified, validated by Safety Manager, and linked to worksite safety plan.

assumptions: Vehicle can reliably estimate performance parameters (cycle time, energy efficiency) from onboard sensors and task type.
Teleoperation module has access to complete worksite mission context (site conditions, task objectives, operator intent).
Safety Services has access to worksite safety plan and can validate performance targets within acceptable latency (<5 min).
NGHE Solution has sufficient computational capacity to perform derivation and baseline management without impacting real-time vehicle control.
Worksite safety plan is available in machine-readable format for Safety Services validation.
Performance targets are derived deterministically from worksite inputs (no stochastic or probabilistic derivation required at this level).
open_risks: Vehicle performance model accuracy and staleness; mitigation: periodic calibration against actual performance data.
Teleoperation mission context completeness and ambiguity; mitigation: structured input schema with validation.
Safety Services validation latency blocking baseline updates; mitigation: asynchronous validation with escalation protocol.
NGHE Solution monolithic design if derivation logic not internally modularized; mitigation: establish sub-module structure.
Interface contract enforcement across Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services; mitigation: strict interface governance.
Baseline versioning and rollback integrity; mitigation: centralized audit trail in NGHE Solution.
evidence_needed: Derivation algorithm specification (e.g., weighted combination formula, input parameters, constraints).
Interface schemas for Tasking (mission context structure) and SafetyAlert (validation result format).
Baseline versioning and rollback procedures (e.g., semantic versioning, rollback protocol).
Calibration trigger conditions and frequency (e.g., performance deviation threshold, periodic recalibration interval).
Safety Manager review and approval workflow for safety-critical performance targets.
Internal sub-module structure within NGHE Solution (e.g., Derivation Engine, Baseline Manager, Traceability Engine).
Test scenarios exercising derivation with various worksite contexts and safety constraints.
Traceability matrix mapping worksite inputs to derived performance targets.
Baseline history audit trail with timestamps, change records, and approval signatures.
